title: The $250,000 FIFA 2026 Package — What You Get When Money Is No Object
excerpt: There's a tier above 'VIP.' It costs a quarter million dollars. And I went inside to see what it actually looks like.
content:
I thought I'd seen the top of the FIFA hospitality pyramid. $50K packages. $87K suites. Private jets and Rolls-Royces.

Then someone told me about the Platinum Presidential package.

$250,000.

For one person.

I had to see it.

## The Initial Contact

It doesn't start with a booking form. It starts with a phone call from the head of concierge services. They want to know what you want before they tell you what they have.

My contact was a woman named Sarah. She spent 30 minutes asking about my preferences:
- Which matches
- Which cities
- Dietary requirements
- Preferred aircraft type
- Hotel brand preferences
- Any guests

Then she said: "I'll put together a proposal. You'll have it in 48 hours."

## What $250,000 Buys

I wasn't the one buying it. But I got a tour of what a Platinum Presidential client would receive.

**Transportation:**
- Gulfstream G700 for the entire tournament (not per flight — YOUR jet for 6 weeks)
- Dedicated crew on standby
- Rolls-Royce Phantom in every city (pre-positioned before you arrive)
- Helicopter transfers for short hops between host cities

**Accommodation:**
- Penthouse suite at the best hotel in each host city
- Private chef on retainer for the duration
- Personal security detail
- Daily housekeeping with laundry service

**Match Access:**
- Same private suite for every match you attend (yours for the tournament)
- Field access for warmups
- VIP parking at every stadium
- Dedicated butler at each venue

**The Extras:**
- Personal concierge on WhatsApp 24/7 (not a team — one person)
- Private events with football legends
- Custom itinerary — change it whenever you want
- Anything else you can think of

## The Tour

I walked through one of the suites reserved for Platinum clients at SoFi Stadium.

It had a private bathroom with a shower. A full dining table for six. A couch that converted into a bed ("for the client who wants to nap between matches," the guide said).

And the view was absolutely unobstructed. 50-yard line. First row of the suite level.

The butler's name was Michael. He'd been assigned to the same suite for the entire World Cup. He knew every detail of the client's preferences before they arrived.

## Who Buys This?

I asked Sarah who the typical Platinum client is.

"High-net-worth individuals who value time more than money. They don't want to make decisions. They want everything handled. They want to show up and enjoy."

She mentioned a Saudi prince who booked it for his family. A tech founder who didn't want to deal with logistics. An oligarch's wife who wanted the full experience.

"We have 6 Platinum slots. 4 are taken. 2 remain."

## Is It Worth It?

Objectively? $250,000 is a life-changing amount of money for 99% of people.

But ask yourself: if you had $50 million, would you rather spend 6 weeks coordinating logistics yourself — or pay someone $250K to make it effortless?

The Platinum Presidential package isn't about luxury. It's about buying back your time.

[Enquire about Platinum Presidential] — serious enquiries only. Sarah will call you.

*I'll never buy it myself. But I'm glad to know it exists. It means the concierge team operates at a level I didn't even know existed.*
